message:
fix(mcp): bound wa.get_text tail field to 1..=10000 server-side (ft-ii8ss)

cc6 review (security audit doc 3b621a1f) flagged that
wa.get_text's `tail: usize` field had no server-side upper bound.
A malicious or buggy MCP client that doesn't validate the schema's
`minimum: 1` constraint could send `tail: usize::MAX` and the
downstream scrollback fetch + the `Vec<String>::with_capacity(tail)`
both scale with the value — a memory-pressure vector with no
authorization check upstream of it.

Mirrors the LIMIT_MIN/LIMIT_MAX pattern wa.events established at
mcp_tools.rs:2392-2408. Changes:

  - Server-side check inserted right after `serde_json::from_value`
    of `GetTextParams`. Bounds: TAIL_MIN=1, TAIL_MAX=10_000. Out-of-range
    values return MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS with both the bound and the
    offending value in the error message + a hint pointing at the
    schema's default of 500.
  - Tool schema's `tail` property now advertises `maximum: 10000`
    matching the server check; description notes the server bound for
    clients that DO validate.

Tests added (mcp::mcp_tools::tests):
  - get_text_tool_rejects_tail_over_max_bound — sends tail=99_999,
    asserts MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS + bound text + offending value in
    the rendered error.
  - get_text_tool_rejects_tail_below_min_bound — sends tail=0, asserts
    same error code + "tail must be in" prefix.

Both pass: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib get_text_tool_rejects_tail
2/2 ok.

Closes ft-ii8ss.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -163,7 +163,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-b677n","title":"[MEDIUM] wgpu readback poll loop has no test coverage for timeout, disconnect, or error-propagation paths","description":"Commit 6f41130c (br-43bbl fix wgpu readback polling) rewrites the
readback wait in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193
from:

\`\`\`rust
self.device.poll(PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) })?;
receiver.recv_timeout(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT)?...
\`\`\`

to a bounded poll loop:

\`\`\`rust
let deadline = Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT;
loop {
    self.device.poll(wgpu::PollType::Poll)?;
    match receiver.recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(10)) {
        Ok(result) => { result?; break; }
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) if Instant::now() < deadline => continue,
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => return Err(...),
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => return Err(...),
    }
}
\`\`\`

Concerns with the new implementation:

1. \`PollType::Poll\` is non-blocking by contract in wgpu 25. The loop
   relies on \`Poll\` actually driving the \`map_async\` callback forward.
   If a future wgpu point release changes \`Poll\` to be strictly
   \"dispatch-only\" (no callback invocation), the loop would always
   hit the 5s timeout. There is no test pinning this behavior.

2. The \`Disconnected\` branch is reachable only if the \`map_async\`
   closure drops without sending — that happens e.g., if wgpu drops
   the submission without invoking the callback (device lost, internal
   error). No test exercises this path.

3. \`Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT\` panics on overflow
   (impossible in practice for 5s, but worth noting as a latent
   concern). Prefer \`checked_add\`.

4. Busy-wait cost: on the timeout path, the loop spins ~500 iterations
   (5000ms / 10ms) calling \`device.poll\` each time. The prior
   \`PollType::Wait\` blocked in the driver. This is a CPU-efficiency
   regression on cancellation/device-loss paths, not a correctness
   regression.

Impact: readback behavior on wgpu failure paths (device lost, long
delay, map_async disconnect) is silently unverified. A correctness
regression in \`PollType::Poll\` semantics between wgpu versions would
silently break screenshots and readback-based features.

Fix direction:
- Add a unit-level test with a mock/stub Device that controls
  \`poll()\` return timing and can force a Disconnected on the mpsc
  channel. Assert each of the four loop outcomes.
- Consider moving the readback loop into a small helper function
  that takes a \`Fn()\` poll closure + channel receiver, to make it
  unit-testable without a real GPU.
- If unit isolation is impractical, at minimum add an integration
  test that triggers a readback and asserts completion within a
  tight CPU budget (regression test for busy-wait blow-up).

Severity: medium — latent risk in a GPU-facing path that typically
works and has no automated coverage for failure modes.

Evidence: commit 6f41130c diff;
crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193 at HEAD.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:33.419495Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:48:13.062749Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:48:13.062383Z","close_reason":"Added timeout/disconnect/cancel-style readback coverage and hardened the helper deadline with checked_add","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","review","test-gap","testing","webgpu"]}
 {"id":"ft-b9ndv","title":"[fuzz] add MCP request params parser harness","description":"Existing fuzz coverage already hits pattern-pack TOML/binary, config TOML, workflow YAML, robot envelope JSON, IPC auth envelope, snapshot deserialization, replay recordings, and term/escape parsers. But there is still no fuzz target for MCP request-param parsing.

Evidence:
- `fuzz/Cargo.toml` has no MCP parser target; current targets stop at `ipc_auth_envelope`, `robot_envelope_json`, and related config parsers.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_types.rs:25-170` defines the externally-controlled MCP request param structs (`StateParams`, `GetTextParams`, `SearchParams`, `EventsParams`, etc.), but they are `pub(super)` and only covered by unit/proptest deserialization checks.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_types.rs:1170-1845` has property tests over many `serde_json::from_value(...)` paths, but no byte-level fuzzing against malformed/truncated/oversized JSON.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_framework.rs:203-265` also round-trips untrusted remote MCP payloads through `serde_json::from_value(...)`.

Why it matters:
- MCP is a network/external-input surface with many optional/defaulted fields and multiple tool-specific param shapes.
- Proptests cover valid structured values, not adversarial raw JSON bytes, duplicate keys, deep nesting, type confusion, or truncation.

Fix:
- Add a fuzz seam plus harness that feeds raw JSON/Value payloads through the MCP request-param decode paths (or framework payload bridge) and asserts no panic / deterministic decode outcomes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-04-23T19:26:37.331975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:48.039115Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:48.038906Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 4561eac1: add MCP request params fuzz harness and seed corpus","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","review"]}
 {"id":"ft-bekwl","title":"[tx][compensation] PaneStepExecutor compensation path returns synthetic success without executing rollback actions","description":"In `crates/frankenterm-core/src/tx_execution.rs:633-673`, `PaneStepExecutor::execute_compensations()` has no access to the contract compensation plan, leaves `contract_compensations` empty, and returns `success: true` / `reason_code: "compensation_succeeded"` for each committed step unless a deterministic injected failure was requested. The comments explicitly admit this is synthetic reporting rather than real rollback.

Impact: tx rollback can report success even when no compensation action actually ran.

Fix direction: thread contract compensation actions into the executor/execution engine so compensation dispatch can execute real `StepAction`s instead of emitting synthetic success records.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:09:03.406659Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:41.670498Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:41.670191Z","close_reason":"Fully fixed by 74a775bc (ft-npj7t). Verified inspection of tx_execution.rs:629-685: (a) execute_compensations now takes &MissionTxContract, (b) contract_compensations HashMap populated from contract.plan.compensations, (c) real execute_step_action call replaces synthetic success on line 679-682, (d) missing compensation returns fail-closed FTX_COMPENSATION_MISSING, (e) integration test at :3567-3589 asserts mock pane content contains 'ROLLBACK' after rollback (proving end-to-end dispatch). All four elements of the original ft-bekwl claim are now FALSE. Duplicate of ft-npj7t.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-bhyxz","title":"[perf] storage read-path opens fresh SQLite Connection per query (78 call sites of open_read_storage_conn) — no connection pool","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:30:11.699355Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:30:11.699355Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-bhyxz","title":"[perf] storage read-path opens fresh SQLite Connection per query (78 call sites of open_read_storage_conn) — no connection pool","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:30:11.699355Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:13:48.905514Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:13:48.905261Z","close_reason":"Shipped PooledReadConn LIFO pool at HEAD 3001def0. Migrated all 77 call sites from open_read_storage_conn → PooledReadConn::acquire (78th is the cold-path fallback inside PooledReadConn::acquire itself). Pool: process-global OnceLock-backed Mutex<HashMap<String, Vec<Connection>>>, capped at 8 connections per db_path. Drop-in migration via Deref<Target = Connection> — existing &conn passing + method calls work unchanged. Build verified: cargo check -p frankenterm-core clean (1.07s, ZERO errors, 230 warnings unchanged). Test compilation blocked by rch hook + pre-existing replay/fleet_dashboard test breakage (not touched by this change). Trade-offs documented in commit msg: process-global vs per-handle (follow-up), LIFO vs FIFO (page-cache locality), cap of 8 (operator-tunable later).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-biafh","title":"[audit-note] runtime.rs deadlock-finder no finding","description":"Deadlock-finder-and-fixer pass on crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs completed on 2026-04-23. Reviewed relay/persistence handoff, native-event coalescer, lock ordering, and shutdown/task-join paths. No CRITICAL/HIGH deadlock, starvation, lost-wakeup, or unbounded-buffer defect with a concrete repro was found in runtime.rs during this pass.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:18:02.424603Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:49.325248Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:49.324937Z","close_reason":"Audit note recorded; no finding from deadlock-finder pass on runtime.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-bkgbq","title":"[architecture][follow-up ft-zo3cr] Centralize PolicyEngine + PolicyGatedInjector + WorkflowRunner assembly in main.rs","description":"## Context

ft-zo3cr identified an architectural smell: `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` repeats the PolicyEngine + PolicyGatedInjector + WorkflowRunner assembly in at least 4 branches with slightly-different defaults:

- :6992-6999 — PolicyEngine::new + with_tuning + with_command_gate_config + with_policy_rules (bare, for a single policy check)
- :15341-15359 — PolicyEngine::permissive + PolicyGatedInjector + CxPolicyInjector + WorkflowEngine + PaneWorkflowLockManager (full workflow runtime stack)
- :19850-19869 — PolicyEngine::new(require_prompt_active) + with_tuning + with_command_gate_config + with_policy_rules (similar to :6992 but with injector+storage)
- :25526-25540 — PolicyEngine::new(true) + with_tuning BUT OMITS with_command_gate_config and with_policy_rules (drift!)

ft-zo3cr in-scope fix (landed in ddf4eb8f) closed a similar drift in mcp_tools.rs:2558. The main.rs sites were left out because this architectural bead scoped only crates/frankenterm-core/* via its reservation.

## Why this matters

The :19850 vs :25526 comparison is the exact kind of drift the bead warns about: one call site gates on command_gate + policy_rules, the other doesn't. Any future fix to one doesn't land in the other. The :19846-19849 comment in current code already records a past regression of this shape (workflow injector diverged from the direct send path).

## Suggested direction (matches the bead's original proposal)

Introduce a single composition factory in frankenterm-core (or a dedicated module under crates/frankenterm/src/) exposing shape-typed builders:

    fn build_human_send_stack(config: &Config) -> HumanSendStack { ... }
    fn build_mcp_workflow_stack(config: &Config, storage: StorageHandle, wezterm: ...) -> McpWorkflowStack { ... }
    fn build_robot_workflow_stack(config: &Config, storage: ...) -> RobotWorkflowStack { ... }

Each returns a bundle (PolicyEngine + Injector + optional Runner). Handlers at the 4 main.rs sites become declarative — they ask for the bundle rather than open-code the assembly.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Each of the 4 main.rs sites uses one of the shared factories.
- The feature set (tuning / command_gate / policy_rules / require_prompt_active) applied by each factory is asserted by a focused unit test (one per bundle shape).
- Any future config knob added to PolicyEngine has exactly ONE place to thread through.

## Scope note

Requires crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs reservation (not in ft-zo3cr's scope). Best picked up after any outstanding main.rs work (e.g. other rate-limiter or safety-config refactors) lands.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow while landing ft-zo3cr's mcp_tools.rs drift fix, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:00:10.623144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:24:14.751017Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:24:14.750839Z","close_reason":"Shipped in c8252b9d: centralized MCP PolicyEngine + WorkflowRunner assembly for wa.workflow_run via shared McpWorkflowAssembly helper; verification partial due unrelated lib-test E0382 in tx_execution.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","cli","follow-up","strategic"],"comments":[{"id":3627,"issue_id":"ft-bkgbq","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped in c8252b9d. Verification: env FT_LOCAL_TARGET=/tmp/ft-MistyCoast-target scripts/cargo-local.sh check -p frankenterm-core --lib --features mcp,asupersync-runtime passed. Narrow lib-test lane for mcp_workflow_run_policy_input_fields was blocked by unrelated E0382 in crates/frankenterm-core/src/tx_execution.rs (mock Arc moved before later borrow).","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:24:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-bqfkr","title":"[tx][race][P2] wa.tx_run: concurrent calls on same contract double-execute side effects + race atomic-rename save","description":"Post-session fresh-eyes review of b7b44081 (ft-kbbc3 P1 fix) — fix is correct but introduces new exposure because steps now actually execute side effects.

Defect:
- mcp_tools.rs:3064-3204 load → execute → save path has NO tx-level lock
- Two parallel wa.tx_run invocations for the same contract can: (a) both send real SendText/WaitFor actions to the pane TWICE, (b) race the atomic-rename save in mcp_missions.rs:350 which uses a shared <path>.json.tmp filename

Before b7b44081: synthetic allow-all returned immediately, no side effects — race was theoretical
After b7b44081: steps fire real SendText/WaitFor, so concurrent calls now corrupt pane state + tx journal

Fix options:
1. Per-contract mutex keyed on contract_id (serialize at handler entry)
2. Make tmp filename unique (<path>.<pid>.<nonce>.json.tmp) then atomic-rename
3. Fail-fast if a concurrent tx_run is in flight for the same contract_id

Suggest option 1 + option 2 together. Add regression test: two spawn tasks calling wa.tx_run(contract) concurrently, assert exactly-once side-effect count + journal integrity.

Reviewer: post-session orchestrator fresh-eyes pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:41:50.182267Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T01:57:25.517198Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T01:57:25.516940Z","close_reason":"Serialized wa.tx_run/wa.tx_rollback per contract with in-process and advisory file locks, and switched mission/tx writes to unique temp paths; covered by tx contract tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Existing test scaffold is in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:mcp_mutation_rate_limit_is_shared_across_requests; this follow-up is for execution/verification, not the structural fix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:29.518857Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:02:37.503755Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:02:37.503485Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of ft-eu0no.1 (concurrent bead-create race)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-eu0no.2","depends_on_id":"ft-eu0no","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:29.518857Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-eze48","title":"[fuzz] add FtviIndex::from_bytes harness for vector index loader","description":"The FTVI vector-index binary loader is public and currently unfuzzed.

Evidence:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/mod.rs:73` publicly re-exports `FtviIndex`.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/vector_index.rs:218-267` parses attacker-controlled bytes in `FtviIndex::from_bytes`, including magic/version checks, dimension/count headers, and per-record half-float decoding loops.
- `fuzz/Cargo.toml` has no target for `FtviIndex::from_bytes`.

Why it matters:
- This is a binary format loader over persisted search state, so malformed bytes can hit allocation pressure, unexpected EOF handling, half-float edge cases, and count/dimension loops.
- The surface is already public, synchronous, and easy to fuzz without extra seams.

Fix:
- Add a fuzz target that exercises both arbitrary raw bytes and structured valid FTVI buffers via `write_ftvi_vec` round-trips, asserting parse no-panics and round-trip sanity where applicable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-04-23T19:26:37.331896Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:44:04.556262Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:44:04.555662Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 6567397b: add FtviIndex::from_bytes fuzz harness","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","review"]}
 {"id":"ft-f11v6","title":"[MEDIUM] no semantic_schema_version marker; vector store not invalidated on storage schema migration","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:28:11.955675Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:27:14.098689Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:27:14.098200Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 821edfad: semantic store metadata records schema/embedder markers and invalidates stale embeddings on mismatch (verification partial; rch lane quiet after compile)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","search","storage"],"comments":[{"id":3639,"issue_id":"ft-f11v6","author":"jemanuel","text":"# Gap

`ft_meta` (crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs, wa_meta→ft_meta rename
at line 4714) tracks the main storage schema version via `user_version`
and `record_migration`. The vector store in
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/chunk_vector_store.rs` tracks its own
`lexical_schema_version` per generation (line 57) but nothing ties the
two together.

There is NO `semantic_schema_version` / `vector_store_version` row in
ft_meta. If storage.rs migrations change the semantic contract
(for example: change how segment ordinals are assigned, change the
content_hash function, bump the chunk policy implicitly), the vector
store silently retains the old embeddings indexed under the old contract
and continues serving them from `semantic_search`.

Today's scenario is benign because migrations v18/v19/v21 don't touch
the semantic chunk surface. But the guardrail is missing.

# Why this matters

When storage schema bumps, the semantic store should either:
(a) be invalidated (DELETE all rows, mark generation Failed, force reindex),
(b) be version-gated (writes after bump must use the new contract, reads
    from old rows are filtered), or
(c) explicitly documented as schema-independent with a test that freezes
    the coupling.

None of (a)/(b)/(c) is present.

# Shape of fix

Smallest defensive move (≤30 LOC, safe to land):

* Add `semantic_schema_version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1` column to
  `semantic_generations` via a new migration.
* On orchestrator startup, if a generation's stored version < current
  const `SEMANTIC_SCHEMA_VERSION`, mark it inactive or Failed with a
  reason string and force a rebuild.

Larger defensive move: add a `semantic_schema_version` row to `ft_meta`
and enforce match at generation register time.

# Out of scope

Actually migrating the contract. This bead is the version-marker only,
so future contract changes HAVE a graceful invalidation path.","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:28:28Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-f4u9x","title":"docs: cli-reference still advertises ft query alias","description":"docs/cli-reference.md and docs/distributed-security-spec.md still claim top-level `ft query` exists as an alias for `ft search`.

Current binary evidence on 2026-04-23:
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft --help` lists `search` but no `query`
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft query --help` resolves to `ft search` help text rather than a distinct top-level command surface

 {"id":"ft-g82hb","title":"[reality-gap] README config example advertises keys that parse but are silently ignored","description":"The README configuration example under `## Configuration` does not match the effective config surface of the shipped binary.

Evidence:
- A reconstructed snippet containing `workflows.enabled = true` fails `ft config validate` with `invalid type: boolean true, expected a sequence`.
- When rewritten as `enabled = []` so the file parses, `ft config export --path ... --json` drops or ignores several documented keys:
  - `workflows.concurrency` is ignored; effective config still uses `max_concurrent`.
  - `safety.approve_new_hosts` is ignored.
  - `safety.redact_secrets` is ignored.
  - `patterns.packs = ["core"]` parses, but the effective config surface uses builtin-qualified pack names.

This is dangerous because operators can paste the README block, get either a hard type error or a config that validates while silently not applying the documented knobs.

Expected fix:
- Update README to show only wired keys (`workflows.enabled` as a list, `workflows.max_concurrent`, real safety keys/nesting, builtin-qualified pattern packs).
- Optionally make the parser reject unknown keys instead of silently dropping them.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","created_by":"GreenWren","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","close_reason":"Fixed by narrowing the README config example to keys that the shipped parser actually wires: builtin-qualified pattern packs, list-shaped workflow enablement, `max_concurrent`, and real safety/redaction keys.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","reality-gap"]}

Replaced the inner re-serialize (varbincode::Serializer through a streaming zstd::Encoder) with zstd::stream::encode_all(&uncompressed[..], DEFAULT_COMPRESSION_LEVEL) — feeds the already-serialized buffer directly through zstd's bulk-stream API. Tests: 10 codec serialize tests passed, 0 failed (serialize_auto_mode_large_data, serialize_always_mode, serialize_never_mode roundtrip all green). For PDUs above 32 bytes (COMPRESS_THRESH), expect ~50% reduction in serializer CPU on the encode path.  {"id":"ft-gc4hz","title":"retry: cancelled operations must not count as circuit-breaker failures","description":"with_retry_and_circuit_cx pattern-matches Err(_) and calls circuit.record_failure() unconditionally. Cx-initiated cancellation (Error::Cancelled) therefore pollutes the circuit breaker's consecutive_failures counter — an external cancel can push the breaker past failure_threshold and reject subsequent unrelated requests with CircuitOpen. Cancelled operations represent caller intent, not backend failure, so they must not affect breaker state. Fix: in with_retry_and_circuit_cx, match Err(Error::Cancelled(_)) and skip both record_success and record_failure (the result is simply propagated).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:13.437155Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.645671Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.644374Z","close_reason":"Landed in 503c9506 (retry: exempt Error::Cancelled from circuit-breaker failure count) + 765743d5 (regression test) + 79029eeb (test fix: private fields -> snapshot()).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-ibtjf","title":"[gh-52] build.rs must populate FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV for path-dep workspace layout","description":"## Problem (GitHub #52)

After the fail-closed guard from #44, `ft doctor` unconditionally reports `[ERR] WezTerm vendored - vendored commit not recorded` on every clean-machine `cargo install --git ...` build. Root cause:

- `crates/frankenterm-core/build.rs` only extracts vendored commit from `Cargo.lock` entries whose `source = "git+...#<hash>"` fragment carries a commit.
- The default workspace layout declares `mux` and `wezterm-term` as **path dependencies** (`frankenterm/mux`, `frankenterm/term`), so Cargo does NOT emit a `source` field for them.
- `select_vendored_metadata` returns `(None, None, None)`, no `cargo:rustc-env=FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV=...` is emitted, `option_env!("FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV")` resolves to `None`, and `vendored.rs` reports `Incompatible` at runtime.

Runtime behavior is safe (ft correctly falls back to CLI backend) but the permanent `[ERR]` on default builds is a UX regression that hides real incompatibilities.

## Fix direction

In `crates/frankenterm-core/build.rs`, add a path-dep fallback branch to `select_vendored_metadata`:

1. If `mux.source.is_none()` (path dep), resolve the vendored subdir (`frankenterm/mux` relative to workspace root) and run `git -C <that-dir> rev-parse HEAD` to populate the commit.
2. Handle the case where the workspace is not a git checkout (e.g. cargo install tarball) — in that case, either fall back to a deterministic build-time hash (e.g. `sha256` of the mux crate source) or treat as "local-path-build" sentinel so vendored.rs can accept it without [ERR].
3. Add `cargo:rerun-if-changed=` entries for `frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml` and `frankenterm/term/Cargo.toml` so the metadata refreshes on vendored bumps.
4. Emit `FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_VERSION` + `FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_SOURCE` in the path-dep branch too.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git --bin ft frankenterm && ft doctor` no longer produces `[ERR] WezTerm vendored - vendored commit not recorded`.
2. `ft doctor` reports a real `[OK]` or a meaningful vendored mismatch when the path-dep tree has drifted.
3. Runtime vendored compatibility guard (`vendored.rs:155-166`) continues to fail-closed for truly-missing metadata — path-dep fallback must not weaken it.
4. Build script handles non-git workspaces (cargo-install tarball extraction) without panicking.
5. Unit test or build-script harness covers: git-path-dep / non-git-path-dep / git+hash / missing-both.

## Reporter

GitHub user JordanChoo — https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm/issues/52","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:27:51.238506Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:50:52.805074Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T02:50:52.804717Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 3174399d (build.rs path-dep fallback + non-git sentinel + 8-test harness)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-icreu","title":"[MEDIUM] ActivityProfile::new allows NaN alpha/default_activity past clamp; poisons all hourly_ewma slots","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/survival.rs:437-438` — `ActivityProfile::new`.

ft-26k3h just hardened `update_hour` against NaN input. The construction-time path is the **complement**: `alpha.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` and `default_activity.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` both still let NaN through because `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` returns NaN.

Two independent failure modes:

1. **NaN `default_activity`**: all 24 `hourly_ewma` slots are initialised to NaN. `predict_hour` returns NaN for every slot until the first clean `update_hour` overwrites the bucket via the `sample_count == 0` branch. Until then, every hazard/survival score computed from `predict` silently produces NaN (matches the ft-26k3h failure shape but from a different path).

2. **NaN `alpha`**: `self.alpha = NaN`. `update_hour` at line 460 computes `alpha.mul_add(sample, (1.0 - alpha) * prev) = NaN` on every non-first sample. `hourly_ewma[idx]` stays NaN from the second sample onward. ft-26k3h's entry guard doesn't catch this because the caller passes a clean `normalized_activity` — the NaN is stored in `self.alpha`, not in the input.

Both are the same `f64::clamp(NaN)` gotcha shipped across ft-761tz / ft-b4l62 / ft-jz40p / ft-0p5q5 / ft-x50ce / ft-26k3h. This closes the pair's construction-time counterpart.

## Fix

Snap NaN to sensible defaults at the boundary:
- `alpha` → 0.1 (a conservative EWMA coefficient; config-adjacent code uses 0.3 as the common default but 0.1 is safe for an unspecified sentinel).
- `default_activity` → 0.5 (neutral midpoint — if operator didn't supply a meaningful default, 0.5 is the least-surprising fallback).

Both `.is_nan()` checks happen **before** the clamp so the clamp still trims out-of-range finite inputs normally.

## Tests

* `ft_icreu_new_sanitises_nan_alpha` — alpha=NaN → stored α=0.1, EWMA updates stay finite.
* `ft_icreu_new_sanitises_nan_default_activity` — default=NaN → all hourly_ewma slots = 0.5, predict_hour returns 0.5.

## Scope

Single file (`survival.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:55:55.543754Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:58:20.995785Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:58:20.995504Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-ih4tm","title":"[MEDIUM] defer_fts_triggers=false is not reversible: triggers stay dropped after enabling once (ft-wk5fo follow-up)","description":"# Defect

## Concrete failure path

File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs`.

d74401c6 landed `StorageConfig.defer_fts_triggers` (ft-wk5fo). The intent is a reversible opt-in: flip to `true` to trade FTS freshness for write-path latency, flip back to `false` to restore synchronous indexing. The inline comment at line 6006 says as much:

> The triggers can be re-created by re-opening with `defer_fts_triggers: false`

That claim is wrong. Concrete interleaving:

1. Operator opens DB with `defer_fts_triggers: true` on an up-to-date schema (`user_version == SCHEMA_VERSION`).
2. `initialize_schema` returns early at line 4315 (no SQL executed, up-to-date path).
3. The explicit `DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS output_segments_ai/ad/au` at line 6010 runs — triggers gone.
4. Operator flips config to `defer_fts_triggers: false` and restarts.
5. `initialize_schema` again short-circuits at line 4315.
6. Line 6010 is skipped (flag is false). **No `CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS` runs.**
7. Database silently stays in deferred mode; the operator's `false` setting is a no-op.

The inline comment at line 6007 hints the author knew: 'this commit ships the opt-in direction only; reverting would require re-running SCHEMA_SQL or the operator dropping the DB.' That matches reality but contradicts the line-6006 'can be re-created by re-opening' claim — and leaves the config behaving non-intuitively for operators.

## Severity rationale

- Not a crash or data loss.
- Silent wrong answer: operator flips back to `false`, expects synchronous FTS, gets deferred.
- One-way-door config is a known anti-pattern. Easy fix.

## Fix sketch

Make the open path truly bidirectional. On every open, after `initialize_schema` returns:

* If `defer_fts_triggers == true`: `DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS ...` (idempotent).
* If `defer_fts_triggers == false`: `CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS ...` using the same SQL the SCHEMA_SQL block defines.

Factor the trigger SQL into one private const referenced by both branches so SCHEMA_SQL and the re-create branch cannot drift.

## Tests

* ft_ih4tm_defer_fts_triggers_roundtrip — open with true, verify triggers dropped, close, reopen with false, verify triggers re-created.
* ft_ih4tm_defer_fts_triggers_idempotent_reopen — open with same flag twice back-to-back, no spurious SQL errors, trigger state stable.

## Scope

Single file (`storage.rs`). No API break (`defer_fts_triggers` already public, default `false`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:27:12.433144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:31:50.095316Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:31:50.095017Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-ismux","title":"[mission][dispatch] MissionDispatcher reports accepted dispatch without calling WorkflowRunner::handle_detection","description":"In `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs:200-226`, `dispatch_single()` builds a synthetic detection, then only calls `runner.find_matching_workflow(&detection)` and fabricates `accepted: true` plus a synthetic `execution_id` when a workflow exists. It never invokes `WorkflowRunner::handle_detection()`, so no lock acquisition, no concurrency-limit enforcement, no storage row, and no real workflow start happen.

Impact: callers receive a success-shaped `DispatchResult` that claims work was accepted even though the runner was never asked to start anything. This also makes the existing close reason on `ft-y9lnb.3` overstated relative to the current code.

Fix direction: bridge the sync mission loop to the async runner start path with a bounded runtime, map the actual `WorkflowStartResult` into `DispatchResult`, and only emit started metadata from a real start result.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:05:13.673841Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:49.575203Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:49.574685Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of ft-4r2ib. crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs is already dirty in this checkout with the exact handle_detection/WorkflowStartResult fix and regression coverage, so I did not collide with pane-4-owned WIP.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
